• Home
  • Bird Flu

H5N1 Bird Flu: Current Situation Summary

Overview

Highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPAI) A(H5N1) virus has been detected in wild birds, poultry, and some mammals, including dairy cattle, in the United States. CDC is monitoring the situation and working with state and local health departments.

The current public health risk to the general population remains low. However, people with close or prolonged exposure to infected animals or contaminated environments are at higher risk.

Epidemiological Summary

Human Cases

As of December 2024, a total of 61 human cases of H5N1 have been confirmed in the United States. Most cases have been associated with exposure to infected poultry or dairy cattle.

Animal Detections

H5N1 has been detected in dairy herds across 15 states. USDA continues to monitor the situation in coordination with state veterinary authorities.

Surveillance Data

Confirmed H5N1 Human Cases by State, 2024
State Total Cases Hospitalized Deaths
Texas 18 3 0
Colorado 12 1 0
Michigan 9 2 0
California 15 4 1
Other 7 1 0

Prevention Recommendations

CDC recommends that people avoid direct contact with wild birds or sick or dead poultry. Farm workers should use appropriate personal protective equipment when working with potentially infected animals.

For Healthcare Providers

Clinicians should consider H5N1 in patients presenting with respiratory illness who have had recent exposure to poultry or dairy cattle. Early antiviral treatment with oseltamivir is recommended for suspected cases.

Laboratory Testing

Wastewater Surveillance Results, December 2024
Region Sites Tested Positive Sites Detection Rate
Northeast 45 3 6.7%
Southeast 38 5 13.2%
Midwest 52 8 15.4%
West 41 7 17.1%

Page last reviewed: December 15, 2024

Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
